archetype: Rhine River Lock Scheduling and Vessel Transit Safety System
domain: Inland Waterway Multinational Port and Lock Authority Operations
description: A real-time deterministic scheduling and safety orchestration system for complex lock chambers on the Rhine, managing vessel convoys, water level balancing, and legacy mechanical gate/valve PLCs across German, French, and Dutch jurisdictions. It is technically hard due to the need for millisecond-precision coordination between heterogeneous SCADA hardware, multilingual operator interfaces, and dynamic constraint-solving for vessel size/type/urgency while meeting strict transnational water level and transit time protocols.
rules: MUST enforce the Central Commission for the Navigation of the Rhine (CCNR) 'Police Regulations for the Navigation of the Rhine' (RPNR) for convoy composition and right-of-way.
MUST integrate with national water level telemetry (PEGEL Online DE/NL) and lock basin volume calculations to prevent hydraulic shock.
MUST support synchronous scheduling across multiple adjacent locks (e.g., Iffezheim, Gambsheim) with dead reckoning for vessel ETA prediction using AIS and radar.
MUST provide a bilingual (DE/FR) or trilingual (DE/FR/NL) operator console with unambiguous command confirmation due to multinational crew and authority overlap.
MUST log all commands and sensor states in an immutable audit trail compliant with EU Regulation (EU) 2019/1239 on European inland waterway transport data.
anti_patterns: DO NOT treat locks as independent nodes—hydraulic coupling between sequential locks requires basin volume forecasting and cannot be scheduled in isolation.

DO NOT rely on vessel self-reported dimensions—must cross-check against official RWS (Rijkswaterstaat) vessel registry via BICS (Barge Information and Communication System) to prevent lock chamber collisions.
DO NOT use standard web-based UI stacks—operator terminals require 24/7 sunlight-readable displays, physical-hardware-button overlay for emergency stop, and sub-200ms command-to-actuator latency.
key_technical_challenges: Hard real-time deterministic control loop interfacing with 30+ year old Siemens/Allen-Bradley PLCs via RS-485 and Profibus with no network jitter tolerance.
Multi-agent constraint optimization for convoy sequencing where a single oversized vessel can block the entire corridor for hours, requiring predictive rerouting to smaller locks.
Sensor fusion failure mitigation—when primary water level pressure transducers fail, the system must interpolate from redundant ultrasonic gauges and historical flow models within 5 seconds.

recommended_stack.language: Rust for safety-critical PLC gateway services and deterministic scheduling engine
recommended_stack.framework: Actix for high-concurrency HTTP/WebSocket operator dashboards with strict latency budgets
recommended_stack.database_engine: TimescaleDB for high-frequency time-series sensor data (1kHz gate position, water level) and event logging
recommended_stack.cache: Redis for in-memory current-state vessel convoy model and lock allocation locks
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka for durable, ordered event streaming between distributed lock control nodes and central planning
special_services: Legacy PLC Integration Service (libmodbus/opc-ua over serial)
Multilingual Speech Synthesis Service for automated bridge/radio announcements per RPNR Art. 4.02
Hydraulic Simulation Microservice (computational fluid dynamics pre-computation for water volume forecasting)

compliance_flags: EU Regulation 2019/1239 (Inland Waterway Transport Data Exchange)
ISO 14726 (Shipboard marking for identification of controls)
National Waterway Authority Safety Directives (e.g., WSV Germany, VNF France)
